hypothesis: If crystal property prediction models integrate complementary information from multiple modalities (graph structure, text descriptions, and XRD spectra) through cross-modal predictive learning in a shared latent space, then they will achieve lower mean absolute error than single-modality baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN) across diverse material property prediction tasks, because each modality captures distinct physicochemical features that collectively provide a richer representation of material structure than any single modality alone.
